title: Mobile physical telemedicine
abstract: A mobile physical telemedicine system includes a processing system and at least one robotic manipulator communicatively coupled to the processing system and configured to support and manipulate a body part of a subject. In some embodiments, the system includes a motion capture system communicatively coupled to the processing system and configured to capture a motion of the subject. In some embodiments, the system includes an adjustable therapy bench. In some embodiments, the system includes a base supporting the at least one robotic manipulator, the motion capture system, and the adjustable therapy bench. In some embodiments, the at least one robotic manipulator is configured to be controlled remotely by a clinical provider. In some embodiments, the at least one robotic manipulator is configured to be operated in an autonomous mode.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A mobile physical telemedicine system, comprising: a processing system; at least one robotic manipulator communicatively coupled to the processing system and configured to support and manipulate a body part of a subject; a motion capture system communicatively coupled to the processing system and configured to capture a motion of the subject; an adjustable therapy bench; and a base supporting the at least one robotic manipulator, the motion capture system, and the adjustable therapy bench. 2. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, wherein the at least one robotic manipulator is configured to be controlled remotely by a clinical provider. 3. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1. wherein the at least one robotic manipulator is configured to be operated in an autonomous mode. 4. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, comprising at least two robotic manipulators, wherein the at least two robotic manipulators are configured to be controlled by a clinical provider. 5. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, comprising a first robotic manipulator and a second robotic manipulator, wherein the first robotic manipulator is configured to be controlled by a clinical provider and the second robotic manipulator is configured to operate in an autonomous mode. 6. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, wherein the at least one robotic manipulator further comprises at least one of a force sensor, a torque sensor, and a current sensor. 7. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, wherein the at least one robotic manipulator is configured to be controlled by impedance control. 8. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, further comprising a microphone and configured to transmit audio data received from the microphone to a computing device of a clinical provider. 9. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, further comprising a camera and configured to transmit data related to communication from the subject and movement of the subject received from the camera to a computing device of a clinical provider. 10. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, wherein the mobile physical telemedicine system is disposed in a vehicle and wherein the base is movable and configured to load the mobile physical telemedicine system in and out of the vehicle. 11. The mobile physical telemedicine system of claim 1, wherein the at least one robotic manipulator is configured to support and manipulate a limb of the subject. 12. A computer-implemented method for providing mobile physical telemedicine to a subject during a telemedicine session, using a mobile physical telemedicine system, the mobile physical telemedicine system including a processing system, at least one robotic manipulator configured to support and manipulate a body part of the subject, the method comprising: detecting, by the processing system, an initiation of the telemedicine session by a remote computing device: receiving, by the processing system, a selection of an operating mode for the at least one robotic manipulator from the remote computing device; operating, by the processing system, the at least one robotic manipulator in accordance with the operating mode to perform at least one physical activity with the subject, the physical activity related to rehabilitation or physical training. receiving, by the processing system, a command to end the telemedicine session from the remote computing device; and terminating, by the processing system, movement of the at least one robotic manipulator. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the operating mode is an autonomous mode. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the operating mode is a teleoperation mode. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the mobile physical telemedicine system includes first and second robotic manipulators and wherein the operating mode for the first robotic manipulator is a teleoperation mode and the operating mode for the second robotic manipulator is an autonomous mode. 16. The method of claim 12, wherein the at least one robotic manipulator is configured to support and manipulate a limb of the subject. 17. The method of claim 12, wherein the processing system is coupled to a network and wherein the processing system receives the selection of an operating mode over the network. 18. The method of claim 14, wherein the processing system is coupled to a network and wherein the processing system receives a plurality of commands to control the at least one robotic manipulator over the network. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein the mobile physical telemedicine system further includes a motion capture system configured to capture a motion of the subject and wherein the method further comprises analyzing, by the processing system, the captured motion. 20. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having software encoded thereon, the software, when executed by a processing system coupled to a mobile physical telemedicine system including at least one robotic manipulator configured to support and manipulate a body part of a subject, operable to: detect an initiation of a telemedicine session by a remote computing device; receive a selection of an operating mode for the at least one robotic manipulator from the remote computing device; 

 operate the at least one robotic manipulator in accordance with the operating mode to perform at least one physical activity with the subject, the physical activity related to rehabilitation or physical training. receive a command to end the telemedicine session from the remote computing device; and terminate movement of the at least one robotic manipulator.
